changes.citation: automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:1-7 — all five required fields present: work_id=ARCH-RT-5C_hypothesis_runtime_promotion, branch=work/ARCH-RT-5C-hypothesis-runtime-promotion, risk_level=HIGH, execution_model=TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH, change_type=MIXED

backend/core/analytics/root_cause_compiler_v1.py:403 — compile_root_cause_v1() is the Intelligence Core output assembly function. backend/core/knowledge/compiled_hypothesis.py:247 — runtime_summary_for_hypothesis() governs the presentation-layer wording emitted from compiled hypotheses. Both files are within the Intelligence Core (SOP §3: analytical pipeline logic, output assembly mechanisms). HIGH is mandatory per SOP §10.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:6 — MIXED. Sprint adds governed content (compiled hypothesis schema enforcement, audit artefacts, runtime promotion report) AND behavioural changes (runtime_summary_for_hypothesis() hardened from fail-open to fail-closed; compile_root_cause_v1() gains pilot-gated compiled routing for signal_vitamin_d_low). Compiled hypothesis content is consumed by the wired compiler path. MIXED governed under BEHAVIOUR controls per SOP §4.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:5 — TWO_PHASE_START_FINISH. Correct: mandatory internal checkpoint (15-point authority preflight + 8-point promotion checkpoint at lines 199-210) must complete before runtime compiler wiring. Kernel start enforces the boundary between preflight and implementation.

All 16 required authority files verified on disk: docs/sprints/healthiq_day_one_architecture_rework_sprint_plan_FINAL_updated.md ✓; docs/architecture/ADR-RT-001 through ADR-RT-004 (all four) ✓; docs/architecture/compiled_hypothesis_contract.md ✓; docs/architecture/compiled_hypothesis_presentation_mapping.md ✓; docs/architecture/ARCH-RT-4_compiled_hypothesis_root_cause_slice_report.md ✓; docs/architecture/ARCH-RT-4_root_cause_divergence_report.md ✓; docs/audit-papers/ARCH-RT-5_M3_hypothesis_root_cause_estate_audit.md ✓; docs/audit-papers/day_one_architecture_launch_readiness_audit.md ✓; docs/audit-papers/active_intelligence_authority_manifest.md ✓; docs/architecture/ARCH-RT-5_full_regeneration_and_launch_gate_report.md ✓; docs/architecture/ARCH-RT-5_split_recommendation.md ✓; knowledge_bus/schema/compiled_hypothesis_schema_v1.yaml ✓; knowledge_bus/compiled/hypotheses/signal_vitamin_d_low.yaml ✓; knowledge_bus/compiled/manifests/arch_rt4_vitamin_d_hypothesis.yaml ✓; docs/governance/KNOWLEDGE_BUS_SOP_v1.3.md ✓; docs/governance/AUTOMATION_BUS_SOP_v1.3.1.md ✓

Non-empty. Promotion checkpoint item 3 (summary_template must exist) is pre-satisfied by the existing artefact.

Manifest resolves; compile_manifest_ref round-trips correctly.

knowledge_bus/compiled/hypotheses/signal_vitamin_d_low.yaml:11-39 — hypotheses list has exactly one entry (hypothesis_id: vitamin_d_nutritional_status_context_v1). One activation_key. No multi-frame selection required. ADR-RT-002 MULTI_FRAME_PER_DIRECTION constraint does not block this pilot. Safe to promote without frame-selection policy.

docs/architecture/compiled_hypothesis_presentation_mapping.md:10-11 — mapping table: physiological_claim → '(not mapped directly)'. summary_template → RootCauseHypothesisV1.summary as PRIMARY runtime wording. Presentation mapping is LOCKED for ARCH-RT-5 pilot (line 2). physiological_claim must not appear as emitted runtime summary text.

docs/architecture/compiled_hypothesis_contract.md:23-26 — legacy loader rejects semver 1.0.0; compiled loader rejects v1 string. backend/core/knowledge/compiled_hypothesis.py:78-79 — validator asserts schema_version == '1.0.0' (SCHEMA_VERSION constant). backend/tests/unit/test_compiled_hypothesis_arch_rt4.py:45-55 — test_loader_fail_closed_invalid_artefact confirms cross-rejection for both directions. Contract preserved.

backend/core/knowledge/compiled_hypothesis_registry_v1.py:32-38 — COMPILED_HYPOTHESIS_PILOT_SPECS is a separate tuple, never added to ROOT_CAUSE_TARGET_SPECS. load_shadow_compiled_hypothesis() at line 48 returns None for non-pilot signal_ids. backend/core/knowledge/root_cause_registry_v1.py:28 — ROOT_CAUSE_TARGET_SPECS is the production registry; it does not import compiled_hypothesis_registry_v1. Separation is enforced.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:217-232 — scope items 1-10 define allowed surface: compiled hypothesis presentation mapping hardening, summary_template mandatory enforcement, runtime_summary_for_hypothesis() fail-closed, compiled hypothesis loader/helper functions, one pilot signal wired into root-cause runtime, legacy YAML fallback preserved, production registry not broadly replaced, shadow comparison / regression tests, direct cross-load / fail-closed boundary tests, runtime promotion report and audit evidence. Out-of-scope list (lines 289-311) prohibits full estate migration, multi-frame promotion, SignalRegistry, SignalEvaluator, PSI, card evidence, frontend, packages, investigation specs, SSOT, thresholds, scoring.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:314-328 — 12 required tests covering: artefact validation, manifest resolution, runtime promotion uses summary_template, missing summary_template fails closed, physiological_claim not emitted, legacy YAML loader still works, compiled loader rejects legacy YAML, legacy loader rejects compiled YAML, pilot root-cause runtime output matches expected, non-pilot pathways unchanged, multi-frame promotion blocked, no raw investigation spec runtime reads. Adequate.

Risk 1: runtime_summary_for_hypothesis() hardening (compiled_hypothesis.py:247-254) changes fail-open to fail-closed. Currently this function is called in the SHADOW path (artefact_as_shadow_dict at line 273). After hardening, shadow path must not regress — vitamin D artefact has summary_template so no test breakage. Risk 2: compile_root_cause_v1() pilot-gated routing must be strictly gated on signal_id == PILOT_SIGNAL_ID (compiled_hypothesis.py:17). Any signal that is not in the pilot list must continue through legacy loader path unchanged. Risk 3: compiled hypothesis hypotheses have no evidence_for_rules / evidence_against_rules (investigation spec fields used by _compile_finding()); the runtime promotion must emit evidence_for/against from the pre-evaluated string lists in the artefact, not re-run rule evaluation. This requires a new compiler bridge function rather than passing compiled artefact through _compile_finding() as-is.

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:333-346 — 12 STOP conditions covering: missing authority files (#1), pilot artefact fails validation (#2), compile manifest doesn't resolve (#3), summary_template missing and cannot be added from governed source (#4), runtime mapping would use physiological_claim (#5), silent fallback to physiological_claim (#6), requires broad compiler redesign (#7), requires multi-frame policy (#8), requires package/spec/PSI/card/frontend changes (#9), legacy YAML unavailable (#10), non-pilot stability unprovable (#11), scope expands to full estate migration (#12). Adequate.

backend/core/knowledge/compiled_hypothesis.py:247-254 — runtime_summary_for_hypothesis() currently returns row.physiological_claim.strip()[:200] when summary_template is absent (fail-OPEN). Carry-forward from ARCH-RT-5 GPT review (prompt lines 146-149) requires: 'runtime_summary_for_hypothesis() fail-safe must fail closed before any compiled hypothesis is promoted. If summary_template is missing, do not silently degrade to physiological_claim.' CURSOR MUST harden this function to raise CompiledHypothesisValidationError (or equivalent fail-closed exception) when summary_template is absent in the promoted runtime context — or refactor the promoted path to call a separate fail-closed function that never touches physiological_claim. The shadow path (artefact_as_shadow_dict) may continue to use a softer surface for comparison purposes. This hardening MUST precede any wiring of compile_root_cause_v1().

knowledge_bus/schema/compiled_hypothesis_schema_v1.yaml:83-88 — summary_template is listed in hypothesis_optional_fields, with no runtime_promotion enforcement rule. Prompt scope item 2 (lines 218-219) and schema requirements (lines 276-284) require: 'mark summary_template as required for runtime-promoted hypotheses, or introduce an explicit promotion_requirements / runtime_promotion rule.' CURSOR MUST update the schema to enforce summary_template for promoted artefacts (e.g., add a promotion_requirements block or move summary_template to hypothesis_required_fields with a note). The existing vitamin D artefact already has summary_template so this change is non-breaking for the current artefact.

backend/core/analytics/root_cause_compiler_v1.py:423-438 — compile_root_cause_v1() iterates _ROOT_CAUSE_TARGETS (legacy YAML loaders only) and calls _compile_finding() for each. There is no compiled hypothesis routing. CURSOR MUST add pilot-gated routing: before or after the legacy loop, detect if target_signal_id is in COMPILED_HYPOTHESIS_PILOT_SPECS (via is_compiled_hypothesis_pilot_signal()), and if so, route to a new compiled bridge function rather than _compile_finding(). The bridge must: (a) load the artefact via get_compiled_hypothesis_artefact(), (b) call the hardened runtime_summary_for_hypothesis() (or equivalent), (c) map artefact hypothesis fields to RootCauseFindingV1/RootCauseHypothesisV1 using the presentation mapping policy (compiled_hypothesis_presentation_mapping.md:8-16), (d) NOT call _compile_finding() which expects legacy evidence_for_rules / evidence_against_rules dict format. Non-pilot signals must continue through the legacy _compile_finding() path unchanged.

backend/core/analytics/root_cause_compiler_v1.py:234-252 — _compile_finding() consumes evidence_for_rules (list of rule dicts with direction/markers_any/markers_all/signals_any keys) and evaluates them via _evaluate_rule(). backend/core/knowledge/compiled_hypothesis.py:CompiledHypothesisRow — evidence_for and evidence_against are Tuple[str, ...] (pre-evaluated string items, not rule dicts). Passing a compiled artefact through _compile_finding() would produce empty evidence lists. The compiled runtime bridge must map CompiledHypothesisRow.evidence_for strings directly to RootCauseEvidenceItemV1(item=..., marker_refs=[]) — pre-evaluated, no rule re-evaluation. This is an architectural incompatibility that requires a separate bridge function, not a workaround inside _compile_finding().

automation_bus/latest_cursor_prompt.md:289-311 — out-of-scope prohibits: full estate migration, legacy YAML deletion, multi-frame promotion, SignalRegistry, SignalEvaluator, PSI artefacts, card evidence artefacts/loader, frontend, package files, investigation specs, biomarker SSOT, clinical thresholds, scoring rails, unit conversion, physiological_claim as retail summary, fallback parsers, helper scripts. Non-negotiable.

backend/core/knowledge/root_cause_registry_v1.py:28 — ROOT_CAUSE_TARGET_SPECS is the sole production registry. backend/core/knowledge/compiled_hypothesis_registry_v1.py:33 — COMPILED_HYPOTHESIS_PILOT_SPECS is the separate pilot set. Prompt scope item 7 (line 226) requires: 'Ensure production registry is not broadly replaced.' The pilot-gated routing in compile_root_cause_v1() must query compiled_hypothesis_registry_v1 only for the pilot signal; it must not merge or replace ROOT_CAUSE_TARGET_SPECS.
